name: Rabies Immunoglobulin (RIG)
description: Exogenous concentrated antibodies providing immediate passive immunity to neutralize the rabies virus at the bite wound entry site before active vaccine-induced antibodies can develop.
preparation: Infiltrate as much of the calculated dose (20 IU/kg for HRIG, 40 IU/kg for ERIG) directly in and around all the animal bite wound sites. Inject any remaining volume intramuscularly at a site distant from the vaccine injection.
safetyAlert: Never inject vaccine and immunoglobulin in the same syringe or at the same anatomical site, as RIG can neutralize the active vaccine antigen.
interactions: Do not exceed standard calculated weight dosage, as excessive RIG can suppress active vaccine antibody development.

demographicCautions.title: PREGNANCY CRITICAL INTERVENTION
demographicCautions.message: Highly recommended and safe in pregnant women. Essential passive immunizer following Category III animal bite exposures to prevent maternal viral entry.
